title: Elor — the World
bodyText:
# The Event (DM Guide) — placeholder name
*(Working title only — NOT "Calamity" (Critical Role). Real name TBD.)*
The **Pool long predates the Event and was NOT its cause.** The Event was a **fast cascade of catastrophes over ~a few weeks**, capped by a **big boom** — a leyline rupture/explosion, gods warring, or the earth partially splitting. That final blast, atop the preceding disasters, **shrouded the sky and forced survivors underground.** **90% of all life died**, most in the first hours; ash choked the plants; centuries of brutal storms followed.

# Topside NOW — not a green recovery (grill 2026-07-09)
The skies cleared ~50 years ago and **living things cling on up there — but it is a strange, hostile place**: dark, ashen, cold, dangerous, prowled by **animals that evolved to survive this twilight world** (mutations, not the old fauna). It is survivable and inhabited, *not* lifeless — but it is also *not* the far green country of the visions. Real restoration hasn't started. **Nobody underground knows even this much.**

# What "Rebirth" actually is
**Releasing [[lathander]]** ([[lathander]]) turns the tide: **sun returns to full glow → ice melts → water washes the ash away → over time, plants return** — and the **nature god** (unnamed — TBD) is finally freed to do their real work instead of the desperate survival-mutations they've been forced to make just to keep anything alive. The old races and creatures can reclaim the surface. Rebirth is a **slow dawn, not a switch.**

> [!dm] Timing (restructure 2026-07-09): **the sun does NOT return in [[arc-chapter-1|Chapter 1]].** Ch 1 ends with the party topside under the **first true moonlight** (dim, freed [[eilistraee]]); finding Lathander's pool — maybe topside — and bringing the dawn is **Chapter 2** ([[arc-chapter-1]]).

# What the people believe (Player Brief)
The surface is lifeless — ash, toxic air — and the unoccupied underground too dangerous. Everyone has their own twisted-by-generations rumor of what happened. [[koviz]] (pop. 2,000–6,000) runs on guild-communalism; survival is the focus; kids are schooled young and slotted into guilds.

> [!dm] Buried hook: **one of the guild leaders KNOWS topside is survivable** — keeps it secret to protect their power, and runs a hidden surface outpost harvesting and studying. Never fired. With [[root-darkpass|Root]] dead, who is it?
>
> Topside imagery seeded: the recurring vision of a river, blue skies, and a **gargantuan tree** (~200ft trunk) — the ranting hobo, the sickness hallucinations, [[kennarea|Kena]]'s spore-visions. (The tree may be the nature god's work / where life clings / the ascent landmark.) Also parked: a "heaven" akin to White Shores / a far green country; the Deadly Sins; "where the wolf goes, the raven follows"; a baby mini-tarrasque; a topside druid circle (Spirit of the Wolf).

Gods once walked; most withdrew to the Outer Planes, occasionally sending **Avatars** (the working theory for Aasimar). The campaign's arc points **up**: Rebirth.
